title: oxalis frutescens
intro: Oxalis frutescens, also known as the lucky clover or wood sorrel, is a low-growing, perennial plant with small, pink or white flowers and heart-shaped leaves that have a sour taste. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Oxalis frutescens include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Oxalis frutescens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can also be used raw in salads or as a garnish.
